rulings.Case Title: ASSET ACCEPTANCE LLC VS. DOROTHY HARRIS et al

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 23, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF ASSET ACCEPTANCE LLC Motion To Set Aside Notice Of Settlement And To Enter Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation And For Entry Of Judgment. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CAROL GILBERT, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO ELLIS O'FARRELL PARKING et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 23, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, ELLIS-O'FARRELL PARKING CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is denied. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether CCSF is estopped from raising the Government Claims Act's claim requirement. See City of Stockton v. Sup. Crt. (2007) 42 Cal.4th 730, 744. The evidence shows that after plaintiff's 1/11/13 "claim" was forwarded to CCSF, CCSF (through the City Attorney's Office) engaged in substantive negotiations regarding the merits of the claim. The 8/28/13 e-mail from CCSF that was forwarded to plaintiff indicates that CCSF treated plaintiff's prior letter/communications as a claim. The court cannot say that plaintiff's counsel as a matter of law acted unreasonably. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CAROL GILBERT, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO ELLIS O'FARRELL PARKING et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 23, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT ELLIS-O'FARRELL PARKING CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is denied. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether Corporation breached the parties' listing agreement. Corporation admits at Request for Admission 44 that it never paid the commission and the evidence demonstrates that plaintiff performed its contractual obligation by obtaining a new tenant. Defendant's moving papers do not sufficiently raise the condition precedent issue and plaintiff may be able to recover a commission under Seck v. Foulks (1972) 25 Cal.App.3d 556, 571. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL WEINBERG ET AL VS. HEALTHNET OF CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 23, 2015, Line 8, DEFENDANT HEALTH NET, INC., HEALTH NET OF CALIFORNIA, INC. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend. As to the third cause of action for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress, the complaint does not adequately allege facts establishing outrageous or extreme behavior. The complaint merely alleges a denial of insurance benefits. "Courts have rejected liability [for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress] where, for example, the insurer simply delayed or denied insurance benefits." (Hailey v. California Physicians' Service (2007) 158 Cal.App.4th 452, 474.) As to the fourth cause of action for Violation of Bus. & Prof. Code sec. 17200, the complaint adequately pleads facts establishing unfair business practices (Complaint, par. 61), but does not set forth class action allegations in compliance with CRC 3.761. Plaintiff is given leave to amend to describe "how the requirements for class certification are met." (CRC 3.761(b).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
